# goalify

> A Claude Code / Agent Skills skill that turns a big coding task into a self-contained `/goal` run
> file. In one session it researches the task, locks the few real decisions (asking the user only the
> genuine forks, one interactive MCQ), and writes one Markdown file whose success criteria are wired to
> commands the run can check, so the user can `/clear` and run the task in a fresh, full-context session
> that verifies every criterion before it finishes. The file deletes itself on success. MIT licensed.

goalify solves a specific, unresolved problem: carrying a plan across `/clear` into a fresh
implementation session (Claude Code issue #32916, "Best practice for plan-to-implementation context
handoff across /clear?", raised on Anthropic's tracker and closed as not planned, with no documented
best practice). It is a two-phase model: PREPARE (research, decide, author the file, in the current
session) then EXECUTE (the user runs `/goal <path>` in a fresh session). It authors the handoff file;
it does not run the task itself. For immediate in-session execution, use autopilot/ultrawork/ralph.

The generated file is hardened with the patterns most autonomous runs miss: a declarative spec,
machine-checkable success criteria wired to named commands, a separate-agent verification pass,
fan-out guardrails (parallel for independent discovery/verify; serialize builds/tests/same-file
writes), output-redirection, anti-placeholder and search-before-assuming rules, a resumable progress
checklist, and a low-freedom gated self-destruct that fires only on full success.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/README.md): What goalify is, who it is for, how to install and use it.
- [SKILL.md](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/skills/goalify/SKILL.md): The skill definition — two-phase model, procedure, the goal-MD template, the one-vs-several decision, the handoff format, and hard rules.
- [Quickstart](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/docs/quickstart.md): Install and run goalify in three steps.
- [LICENSE (MIT)](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/LICENSE): Permissive MIT license.

## Install

goalify ships as a Claude Code plugin (in the 10x marketplace) and as a drop-in skill. Either way you
get `/goalify`, which authors the run file; you execute it with Claude Code's built-in `/goal <path>`
command (Claude Code 2.1.139+, https://code.claude.com/docs/en/goal).

- [Plugin (recommended)](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify#quick-start--plugin-install-recommended): `claude plugin marketplace add Aboudjem/10x` then `claude plugin install goalify@10x`.
- [Manual / skill-only](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify#install-manual--skill-only): `git clone https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify`, then copy `skills/goalify` into `~/.claude/skills/`. Then say "goalify this: <task>" in Claude Code.

## Skill

- [Skill directory](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/tree/main/skills/goalify): The full skill (`SKILL.md`).
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Instructions for AI agents using this repo, including the rules an agent MUST honor.

## Examples

- [Worked example goal file](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/examples/sample-goal-file.md): A real (redacted) `/goal` file goalify produced.

## Optional

- [Evals](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/tree/main/evals): The deterministic check (`check_skill.py`), behavioral scenarios, and the recorded RED→GREEN baseline on Haiku/Sonnet/Opus. Skip if a shorter context is needed.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/Aboudjem/goalify/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release history. Skip if a shorter context is needed.
